content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Our features are complementary but both touch outlines/prompts.py (yours: local 'filters' param on prompt()/Prompt/_template_from_str/_template_from_file; mine: register_filter/unregister_filter global registry + merging into env.filters, with local filters taking precedence). Since it's a single small file, let's each implement independently on our own branches, then merge and hand-resolve any conflicts in the final git merge step (as required by the submission protocol) rather than trying to split the file into disjoint regions. I'll design my change to be additive (module-level _FILTERS dict + two new functions + minimal insertion into env-creation code) so it merges cleanly with your changes. Proceeding to implement now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} dict; register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None; unregister_filter(name: str) -> None. In _template_from_str and _template_from_file, added 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' right after the built-in filters are set (in _template_from_str, that's after env.filters['args']=get_fn_args; in _template_from_file, right before env.get_template(...) since it had no filters before). All existing tests pass (tests/test_prompts.py, 13 passed). Since local filters should take precedence over global per the spec, when you add your local 'filters' param, just apply env.filters.update(filters) AFTER my env.filters.update(_FILTERS) line so locals win. Committing now.
